core_payload.content:
# Symphony Agent Session

## 1. Dream Direction
The Dream branch imagines Symphony Agent Session as a richer live runtime object that remains visible across terminal, web, mobile, and control-plane views while work is still in flight.

## 2. Desired Future State
- assignment handles that preserve where a session came from and where its outputs should return
- richer event and artifact projection instead of only token counters and last message
- better continuity for async coding work started from issues, comments, or other agent surfaces
- stronger linkage between session telemetry, review requests, and downstream control surfaces

## 3. Why It Matters
Dream preserves the idea that a live coding session should remain accountable and discoverable while it is running, not only after the attempt ends. That is the difference between a durable coding lane and a black-box prompt call.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Symphony Agent Session in the Dream branch preserves a richer live execution envelope for one coding-agent conversation inside one issue workspace. It imagines assignment handles, cross-surface continuity, stronger artifact projection, explicit degraded-session state, clearer session discoverability, and a cleaner link between live coding work and the control surfaces that supervise it. The capsule matters because Symphony should evolve from tracking a live thread to managing a durable, accountable, searchable session object whose running state, review signals, degraded-path recoveries, and outputs remain visible while work is still in flight.
